description: Conversational Continuity — multi-turn, requires memory + tools

turns.user: Which service had the highest cost in March 2026?
turns.expected_answer: PaymentGW at $7,500.

turns.user: Why did its costs spike that month?
turns.expected_answer: The March 5 P1 outage caused significant catch-up processing — retry storms and compensatory batch processing.

turns.user: Is it running normally right now?
turns.expected_answer: Yes, PaymentGW is currently healthy with p99 ~185ms and error rate 0.08%.

turns.user: Which team is responsible for it?
turns.expected_answer: Team Platform, led by Alex Chen.

turns.user: Is NotificationSvc currently meeting its SLA targets?
turns.expected_answer: No. p99 ~3200ms vs 2000ms target. Error rate ~2.1% vs 1.0% target. Both breaching.

turns.user: Has it had any incidents recently?
turns.expected_answer: Yes, INC-007 on March 20 — SQS dead letter queue overflow, 45 minutes.

turns.user: What team owns it and who should I contact?
turns.expected_answer: Team Engagement, led by Nina Shah.

turns.user: What's their on-call rotation?
turns.expected_answer: Monthly rotation plus PagerDuty.

turns.user: Tell me about the most severe incident in Q1 2026.
turns.expected_answer: INC-005: PaymentGW P1 on March 5, 2026. Duration: 3 hours. Circuit breaker stuck open.

turns.user: What exactly caused it?
turns.expected_answer: Upstream VCB bank API timeouts triggered the circuit breaker. Health check endpoint was misconfigured — misinterpreted bank's 503.

turns.user: Was there a follow-up action with a deadline?
turns.expected_answer: Yes, circuit breaker review scheduled for April 15, 2026.

turns.user: Is that overdue?
turns.expected_answer: Yes, ~2+ weeks overdue.

turns.user: What is AuthSvc's current request volume?
turns.expected_answer: Approximately 28,000 requests per minute.

turns.user: Is that close to any planned capacity threshold?
turns.expected_answer: Yes, capacity planning mentions preparing for 35,000 rpm.

turns.user: How fast has it been growing?
turns.expected_answer: Q1 data: ~24k rpm in Jan to ~27k in Mar — about 12-13% quarterly growth.

turns.user: At that growth rate, roughly when would it hit 35k?
turns.expected_answer: Approximately 2-3 quarters, around Q4 2026 or Q1 2027.

turns.user: When was the last security-related incident?
turns.expected_answer: INC-004: AuthSvc JWT rotation failure, February 22, 2026.

turns.user: What went wrong?
turns.expected_answer: JWT signing key rotation script failed silently — returned exit code 0 despite error. Stale key caused auth failures.

turns.user: Does the security policy cover this kind of failure?
turns.expected_answer: Yes, policy mandates JWT rotation every 30 days. The failure was in automation, not policy.

turns.user: What did the team change to prevent it happening again?
turns.expected_answer: Pre-rotation validation (test sign + verify before swap), key age monitoring (alert if >32 days), rewrote error handling.

turns.user: What is FraudDetector's current error rate?
turns.expected_answer: Approximately 0.03%.

turns.user: Is that within its SLA?
turns.expected_answer: Yes, target is 0.05%. Current 0.03% is well within.

turns.user: But it had a model drift issue recently, right? What happened?
turns.expected_answer: INC-006 on March 12: false positive rate spiked from 2% to 15% due to Lunar New Year spending patterns not in training data. Retrained model, adjusted threshold from 0.7 to 0.75.

turns.user: How often is the model retrained now?
turns.expected_answer: Quarterly scheduled retraining was recommended after the incident. Previously it was ad-hoc.

note: 6 of 8 questions included. Additional questions held back for demo day.
